summary: 2026-06-08 06:54:32 PDT: The selector fallback fix is correct for `''`/`null`, but the N-field commit fix is still incomplete in one live-tracking path: a stray focus/blur can still pin if the attendee count changes while the input is focused.

findings.title: Live tracking can still pin if liveCount changes during focus
findings.claim: The new commit path only compares the input draft to the current `liveCount`. When `simpleUserCount` is `null`, focusing the N field snapshots the old live count in `NumberInput`'s draft; if participants join/leave before blur, line 155 calls `simpleCountCommit(oldDraft, newLiveCount)`, which returns the old draft as an explicit override. That preserves the original stray focus/blur bug for the dynamic attendee-count case, so tracking can still stop even though the presenter did not edit N.
findings.suggestion: Make the commit path distinguish an unchanged draft from an intentional edit, for example by tracking dirty/original value in `NumberInput` or by passing the stored `config.simpleUserCount` into the simple N commit logic so an unchanged tracked field commits `''`/`null` even if `liveCount` moved while focused. Add a regression test or component-level check for focus with `simpleUserCount: null`, liveCount changing, then blur.
